_comment: mEditEcv LatentSync VIDEO-TO-VIDEO re-lipsync (self-authored ComfyUI API-format graph; not copied source). Given an I2V-generated shot VIDEO + a voiceover audio, re-synthesize the mouth so lips match the audio. Nodes verified against ShmuelRonen/ComfyUI-LatentSyncWrapper (LatentSyncNode, VideoLengthAdjuster) and Kosinkadink/ComfyUI-VideoHelperSuite (VHS_LoadVideo, VHS_VideoCombine). DIFF vs the proj copy comfyui_workflows/latentsync_lipsync.json: proj node 10 is `LoadImage` feeding VideoLengthAdjuster mode=loop_to_audio (that is the SINGLE-STILL-IMAGE path — it fakes a video from one photo and does NOT accept a real video). Here node 10 is `VHS_LoadVideo` (decodes a real mp4 to a frame batch) and node 20 mode=normal (keep the source motion, only reconcile length to audio). PLACEHOLDERS: __IMAGE_FILENAME__ now carries a VIDEO filename (e.g. source_video.mp4 — it MUST have a video extension so VHS_LoadVideo loads it); __AUDIO_FILENAME__=voiceover; __OUTPUT_PREFIX__=output name; __FPS__=pipeline fps (provider replaces the string "__FPS__" with an INTEGER — never a quoted string). We standardize the whole graph on one __FPS__ (load force_rate = adjuster fps = combine frame_rate) so audio/video stay in sync; LatentSync-1.6 is trained at 25fps, so if the pipeline fps (COMPOSE_FPS=30) hurts quality, hardcode 25 on nodes 10/20/40. TO WIRE INTO proj (do NOT do it this round — see handoff): (1) drop this file over comfyui_workflows/latentsync_lipsync.json; (2) provider runpod_serverless_digitalhuman_provider.py: change _IMAGE_NAME from 'presenter.png' to 'source_video.mp4'; (3) gen_lipsync_video.py: feed the shot's I2V video bytes (shot.video_asset_id) as `image=` instead of the presenter still. No _fill_placeholders change is needed because __IMAGE_FILENAME__ is reused.

_meta.title: load source shot video (I2V mp4) -> frame batch
reconcile source frame count to voiceover length (mode=normal keeps real motion)
LatentSync-1.6 re-lipsync (weights baked in image; no cold-start download)
combine lipsynced frames + voiceover -> mp4 (returned as base64 via patched handler)
